query: Naoki Tamura BOJ board rate hike cadence neutral level 2 percent June 2026 statement

{"query":"Naoki Tamura BOJ board rate hike cadence neutral level 2 percent June 2026 statement","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1664,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.cmegroup.com/education/events/econoday/663425","title":"JP: Bank of Japan Announcement","content":"Jan 22, 2026 — In July 2024, the board voted 7 to 2 to hike the policy rate to 0.25% from a range of 0% to 0.1%. Definition. The Bank of Japan is the central ...Read more
The Bank of Japan's nine-member board decided in an 8 to 1 vote to leave the target for the overnight interest at 0.75% after conducting its first rate hike in six meetings in December by raising it by 25 basis points (0.25 percentage point) to a 30-year high.
In the December rate hike statement, the bank stressed that real interest rates would remain significantly negative and thus that accommodative monetary conditions should continue and support economic activities.
The board's median GDP projection was revised up slightly to 1.0% for fiscal 2026 from 0.7% projected in October as Japan's economy has shown some resilience despite easing but still high costs of living and the drag from stiff Trump tariffs.
Given the resilient economy despite the Trump tariff storms, the board’s median GDP projection is likely to be revised up slightly to around 1.0% for fiscal 2026 from 0.7% projected in October while the 1.0% growth forecast for 2027 is expected to be unchanged.
The board holds eight two-day Monetary Policy Meetings a year, in January, March, April, June, July, September, October and December.
Market participants closely monitor the news conference by the BoJ governor that usually starts at 1530 JST (0130 EST/0230 EDT/0630 GMT), a few hours after the bank releases its policy decision."},{"url":"https://www.japantimes.co.jp/business/2026/06/25/economy/boj-hawkish-board-member-more-rate-hikes/","title":"Rate hikes needed every few months, hawkish BOJ board member says - The Japan Times","content":"The Bank of Japan needs to raise interest rates every few months and should quicken the pace in the face of stronger upside inflation risks, Naoki Tamura, one of the most hawkish board members, has said."},{"url":"https://www.tradingview.com/news/forexlive:0c5f70214094b:0-boj-tamura-says-neutral-rate-around-2-and-its-important-to-get-closer-sooner/","title":"BoJ Tamura says neutral rate around 2% and its important to get closer sooner — TradingView News","content":"--- BOJ board member Tamura said underlying inflation has already reached 2% and called for rate hikes every few months toward a 2% neutral rate, adding the BOJ should not hesitate to accelerate if upside price risks heighten.
Tamura said his view is that underlying inflation has already reached 2%, and that upside price risks exist regardless of Middle East developments, per his remarks
Bank of Japan board member Naoki Tamura delivered his most explicit public case yet for an accelerated tightening cycle on Wednesday, calling for rate hikes at a pace of once every few months toward a neutral rate of around 2% and warning that delaying the journey risks forcing the central bank into sharper moves later."},{"url":"https://www3.nhk.or.jp/nhkworld/en/news/20260625_19/","title":"BOJ policymaker calls for faster rate hikes if needed | NHK WORLD-JAPAN News","content":"The BOJ raised its policy rate from around 0.75 percent to 1 percent on June 16.
But Tamura said that the neutral interest rate level, which would neither stimulate nor cool down the economy, would be around 2 percent.
He said that to ease price increases, it would be necessary to raise interest rates by 0.25 percentage points every few months toward that level."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Naoki Tamura BOJ board rate hike cadence neutral level 2 percent June 2026 statement","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www3.nhk.or.jp/nhkworld/en/news/20260625_19","title":"BOJ policymaker calls for faster rate hikes if needed | NHK WORLD-JAPAN News","content":"A Bank of Japan policymaker has indicated that the central bank needs to raise its key interest rate every few months following this month's rate hike. Tamura Naoki, a member of the BOJ Policy Board, said that the bank may also need to accelerate the pace of interest rate hikes depending on future inflation trends. Tamura also indicated that upside risks to inflation are high. But Tamura said that the neutral interest rate level, which would neither stimulate nor cool down the economy, would be around 2 percent. He said that to ease price increases, it would be necessary to raise interest rates by 0.25 percentage points every few months toward that level. Tamura also stressed the need to accelerate the pace of interest rate hikes depending on how prices change. He specifically said that the central bank must not hesitate to increase both the frequency and the size of hikes if the likelihood of upside inflation risks materializing rises.","score":0.8524574,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.japantimes.co.jp/business/2026/06/25/economy/boj-hawkish-board-member-more-rate-hikes","title":"Rate hikes needed every few months, hawkish BOJ board member says","content":"Bank of Japan board member Naoki Tamura is a widely known hawk on the board who frequently calls for interest rates to be raised to a more normal setting. Image 10: Bank of Japan board member Naoki Tamura is a widely known hawk on the board who frequently calls for interest rates to be raised to a more normal setting. The Bank of Japan needs to raise interest rates every few months and should quicken the pace in the face of stronger upside inflation risks, Naoki Tamura, one of the most hawkish board members, has said. “What I envisage as a baseline path is raising the interest rate by a quarter percentage point at intervals of a few months toward the neutral interest rate level of 2%,” Tamura said in a speech Thursday at a meeting with local businesses in Hyogo Prefecture. Tamura is a widely known hawk on the board who frequently calls for raising interest rates to a more normal setting.","score":0.8467682,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/hawkish-boj-policymaker-calls-rate-hike-once-every-few-months-2026-06-25","title":"Hawkish BOJ policymaker calls for rate hike once every few months","content":"## Browse World. ## Browse Business. KOBE, Japan, June 25 (Reuters) - The Bank of Japan should ​raise interest rates once every few months and stand ready to speed up the pace of hikes, hawkish board member Naoki ‌Tamura said, highlighting the bank's focus on inflationary risks from the Middle East conflict. The remarks came in the wake of the central bank's decision this month to raise its policy rate to a 31-year high of 1%, as the Iran war-induced energy shock adds to price pressures from a tight labour market and rising import costs from a ​weak yen. \"Considering the recent increase in upside risks to prices, what I envisage as a baseline path is raising the policy interest rate by 0.25 ​percentage points at intervals of a few months toward the neutral interest rate level of 2%,\" Tamura said in a speech to business leaders in Kobe, western Japan. *   About Reuters, opens new tab.","score":0.7380092,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.tradingview.com/news/forexlive:0c5f70214094b:0-boj-tamura-says-neutral-rate-around-2-and-its-important-to-get-closer-sooner","title":"BoJ Tamura says neutral rate around 2% and its important to get closer sooner — TradingView News","content":"Tamura is the most hawkish voice on the BOJ board and his comments will be read as the outer boundary of where policy could go rather than consensus, but the specificity of the 2% neutral rate target and the every few months cadence language directly echoes what appeared in the June Summary of Opinions, meaning he is not an outlier on direction, only on pace. His dissent on the JGB taper pause is the most market-sensitive element for the long end of the curve: a board member actively pushing for faster balance sheet reduction, framing the pause as a mistake, keeps upward pressure on super-long JGB yields and complicates the BOJ's communication that the halt was a stability measure rather than a policy retreat. --- BOJ board member Tamura said underlying inflation has already reached 2% and called for rate hikes every few months toward a 2% neutral rate, adding the BOJ should not hesitate to accelerate if upside price risks
